name: GitGuardian API
what_it_does: Secret detection and remediation platform with REST API for scanning code repositories, CI/CD pipelines, and historical commits for exposed credentials, API keys, and sensitive data.
use_cases: Scanning pull requests and commits for secret leaks in CI/CD pipelines via API
Querying incident reports for exposed secrets across all monitored repositories
Automating secret remediation workflows by triggering rotation on detection
Monitoring historical repository exposure to identify secrets committed in the past
Integrating with incident management to route secret alerts to responsible teams
not_for: Real-time application-level secrets management (use HashiCorp Vault instead)
SAST/code quality analysis beyond secret detection
Organizations only needing pre-commit hooks without SaaS monitoring
best_when: An agent enforces secret hygiene by scanning code on every commit and automating remediation workflows when credentials are detected.
avoid_when: You only need a local pre-commit hook — use the free ggshield CLI without the SaaS platform.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens from GitGuardian dashboard with configurable scopes (scan, incidents, sources). API key passed as Authorization: Token header. Service account tokens for CI/CD automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer (free): unlimited public repository scanning; 1 developer
pricing.paid_tiers: Team from $99/month; Business from $399/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier for individual developers with public repo monitoring. Paid plans for private repos, team features, and policy management. ggshield CLI is free for local use.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published. REST API well-documented with examples. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Scan API returns structured policy break information with secret type and context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scan API is stateless — same content always produces same result. Incident status updates are idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Scan API returns policy_breaks — agents must check for empty list to confirm no secrets found
Historical scan of large repos is resource-intensive — use incremental scanning for CI
Incident lifecycle (TRIGGERED, ASSIGNED, RESOLVED, IGNORED) must be tracked by agents
EU data residency means API endpoint is api.gitguardian.com — no US residency option

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliant. EU-based data residency. Scoped API tokens. Ironic self-awareness: a secret detection service with strong credential hygiene. Detected secrets are stored encrypted.
